name: Deianira
description: Deianira, daughter of King Oeneus of Calydon and Althaea (or Dionysus in some versions), was a prominent figure in Greek mythology, primarily known as the wife of the hero Heracles. Her story is deeply intertwined with Heracles' labors and tragic demise. Known for her beauty and gentleness, Deianira's life was marked by both love and sorrow. She was initially pursued by the river god Acheloos, but Heracles intervened, wrestling Acheloos and winning her hand in marriage. Their union was initially happy, but Heracles' constant adventures and infidelities cast a shadow over their relationship. The most significant event in Deianira's life was her unwitting role in Heracles' death. While crossing the river Evenus with the centaur Nessus, Nessus attempted to violate her. Heracles shot Nessus with a poisoned arrow. As he lay dying, Nessus tricked Deianira, telling her to collect his blood as a love potion to ensure Heracles' fidelity. Years later, suspecting Heracles of infidelity with Iole, Deianira smeared a robe with the supposed love potion. However, the blood was actually a potent poison from the Hydra's venom that had tainted Heracles' arrows. When Heracles donned the robe, the poison burned him intensely, leading to his agonizing death. Overwhelmed with grief and remorse, Deianira took her own life, unable to bear the weight of her actions. Her story serves as a cautionary tale about jealousy, deception, and the unintended consequences of good intentions.
subtitle: The Unwitting Destroyer of Heracles
achievements: Wife of the great hero Heracles
Mother of Hyllus and other children
Symbol of tragic love and unintended consequences

keyMyths.title: The Centaur Nessus
keyMyths.description: While crossing the river Evenus, the centaur Nessus attempts to violate Deianira. Heracles shoots him with a poisoned arrow, but Nessus deceives Deianira before dying.

keyMyths.title: The Poisoned Robe
keyMyths.description: Jealous of Iole, Deianira smears a robe with Nessus's blood, believing it to be a love potion. The robe poisons Heracles, leading to his agonizing death.

keyMyths.title: Deianira's Suicide
keyMyths.description: Overwhelmed with grief and remorse for her role in Heracles' death, Deianira takes her own life, unable to bear the burden of her actions.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive summary of Greek myths and legends.
sources.text: A comprehensive summary of Greek myths and legends.
